---
title: "Containerized Development"
description: |
  Shoo those dependency problems away with containers!
author:
  - name: TJ Palanca
    url: https://www.twitter.com/tjpalanca
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  distill::distill_article:
    self_contained: false
---

## Useful Code

### General application run test 

Sometimes, you have a dockerized application and want a quick and dirty 
way to test whether it runs on a persisted basis. You can use this
snippet in a Makefile to run on your CI system:

```Makefile
# Test if the container runs
docker-test:
	@docker run -it --name $(APP_NAME) -d $(TEST_ENV) $(REPO_VER) && \
	echo "Waiting 15 seconds for app startup" && sleep 15 && \
	echo "Docker Logs ==========" && docker logs $(APP_NAME) && \
	echo "======================" && \
	docker inspect -f {{.State.Running}} $(APP_NAME) && \
	docker stop $(APP_NAME) || docker stop $(APP_NAME) && \
	docker rm $(APP_NAME) || docker rm $(APP_NAME)
```

What this does is:

1. Run the persistent application in detached mode
2. Waits a specified number of seconds (15 above) 
3. Prints out the logs of the docker image regardless of whether or not
   the container ran successfully
4. Checks whether the container is still running, and fails if not
5. Stops and removes the contianer to clean up regardless of success
   or failure.
